SEED-AML-section_22_suspicious_transacti.raw_text: Banks shall monitor customer transactions for unusual patterns or sudden spikes in volume. Any suspicious transaction must be reported to the Financial Intelligence Unit - India (FIU-IND) within 7 days of arriving at a conclusion that the transaction is indeed suspicious or relates to proceeds of crime.

SEED-AML-section_24_cash_transaction_rep.raw_text: All cash transactions exceeding INR 10 Lakh or its equivalent in foreign currency, or a series of integrally connected cash transactions that aggregate to more than INR 10 Lakh in a single calendar month, must be reported to FIU-IND via Cash Transaction Reports (CTR) by the 15th of the succeeding month.

SEED-AML-section_30_watchlist_and_sancti.raw_text: Banks must run real-time sanctions screening of all customer names, beneficial owners, and cross-border transaction counterparties against the consolidated United Nations Security Council (UNSC) sanctions list, OFAC list, and local watchlists before onboarding and processing transactions.

SEED-AML-section_35_transaction_monitori.raw_text: All transaction alerts generated by the automated AML monitoring system must be investigated and closed within 30 days of alert generation. Alerts flagged as high-risk require immediate escalation to the Principal Officer for further investigation.

SEED-FOREX-section_10_reporting_of_liber.section_title: Section 10 - Reporting of Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS)
SEED-FOREX-section_10_reporting_of_liber.raw_text: All remittances made under the Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS) by resident individuals exceeding USD 250,000 per financial year require prior approval from the Reserve Bank of India. Transaction details and remittances must be reported in Form LRS on a monthly basis by the 10th of the succeeding month.

SEED-FOREX-section_16_foreign_travel_car.raw_text: Authorised Dealers may issue foreign travel cards to resident individuals traveling abroad up to the limit of USD 250,000 or equivalent per financial year. Loading or reloading of travel cards beyond this limit must be blocked automatically by the card issuance system.

SEED-FOREX-section_18_external_commercia.raw_text: Any External Commercial Borrowings (ECB) raised by eligible Indian entities must be reported to the Reserve Bank of India in Form ECB within 7 days from the end of the month in which the loan agreement is signed to obtain an LRN (Loan Registration Number).

SEED-FOREX-section_25_export_proceeds_re.raw_text: The realization and repatriation of export proceeds to India must be completed within 9 months from the date of export. For status holder exporters, the period of realization is 12 months from the date of export, unless extended by the Reserve Bank of India.

SEED-KYC-paragraph_38_customer_due_dilig.section_title: Paragraph 38 - Customer Due Diligence for Individuals
SEED-KYC-paragraph_38_customer_due_dilig.raw_text: Banks shall carry out Customer Due Diligence by obtaining the following documents from individual customers at the time of account opening. A certified copy of any one document notified by the Central Government as proof of identity along with one document as proof of address is mandatory. Permanent Account Number or Form 60 as defined in Income Tax Rules shall be obtained compulsorily. Aadhaar number shall be obtained from customers who voluntarily provide it. No account shall be opened in anonymous or fictitious names. The identity verification must be completed within 24 hours of account opening initiation.

SEED-KYC-paragraph_45_periodic_updation_.raw_text: Regulated entities shall ensure periodic updation of KYC records for all customers. The frequency of updation shall be at least once in two years for high risk customers, at least once in eight years for medium risk customers, and at least once in ten years for low risk customers. Banks shall obtain fresh KYC documents from customers when their risk profile changes from lower to higher category. Failure to complete periodic KYC updation shall result in partial freezing of the account whereby credits shall not be permitted. Only debit transactions essential for the livelihood of the customer shall be allowed after partial freeze. A notice period of thirty days shall be given to the customer before imposing partial freeze.

SEED-KYC-paragraph_56_video_customer_ide.raw_text: The Video Customer Identification Process may be carried out by a bank official using live video. The process shall include a live photograph of the customer, capture of the PAN card, and an OTP sent to the customer's mobile number registered with Aadhaar. The bank official conducting the V-CIP must be a trained employee. All V-CIP sessions must be recorded and stored for a minimum period of six months. The video quality must be sufficient to identify the customer clearly. Customers whose Aadhaar is not seeded with a mobile number shall not be eligible for V-CIP.

SEED-KYC-paragraph_65_central_kyc_record.raw_text: Banks shall register customers on the Central KYC Records Registry for all accounts opened on or after January 1, 2017. A unique fourteen digit KYC Identifier shall be issued to each customer registered on the CKYC Registry. Banks downloading KYC records of existing customers from the CKYC Registry shall carry out fresh physical verification of identity and address documents at the time of download. Physical re-verification of original documents is mandatory regardless of the availability of digital records in the Registry. No exemption from physical re-verification shall be granted to any category of customer including those with V-CIP history.

SEED-KYC-paragraph_72_enhanced_due_dilig.section_title: Paragraph 72 - Enhanced Due Diligence for High Risk Customers
SEED-KYC-paragraph_72_enhanced_due_dilig.raw_text: Banks shall apply enhanced due diligence measures for all customers classified as high risk. High risk customers include Politically Exposed Persons and their family members and close associates. Enhanced due diligence shall include obtaining source of funds documentation, senior management approval for account opening, and enhanced ongoing monitoring at quarterly intervals. Banks shall not open accounts for foreign nationals from high risk jurisdictions without prior approval from the Compliance department head. All high risk account opening decisions must be documented with specific reasons recorded.

SEED-KYC-paragraph_89_record_keeping_and.section_title: Paragraph 89 - Record Keeping and Reporting Obligations
SEED-KYC-paragraph_89_record_keeping_and.raw_text: All records relating to the identity of customers and their transactions shall be maintained for a minimum period of five years after the business relationship has ended. Transaction records exceeding the prescribed threshold of ten lakh rupees shall be reported to the Financial Intelligence Unit within seven working days. Suspicious transaction reports shall be filed within seven working days of the transaction being identified as suspicious. Banks shall not tip off the customer regarding the filing of any suspicious transaction report. All KYC records must be made available to the authorities within forty eight hours of request.

SEED-CYBERSECURITY-section_5_multi_facto.raw_text: Banks must implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all administrative access to critical servers and customer-facing digital banking portals. MFA must include at least two independent factors: knowledge (password), possession (OTP/Token), or inherence (Biometrics) to protect customer sessions.

SEED-CYBERSECURITY-section_8_data_encryp.raw_text: All customer personal data and transaction data must be encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher protocols, and at rest using AES-256 or higher encryption algorithms. Encryption keys must be rotated at least once every 12 months to prevent unauthorized disclosure.

SEED-CYBERSECURITY-section_14_incident_r.raw_text: Any cyber security incident, security breach, or unauthorized access to bank databases must be reported to the RBI and CERT-In within 6 hours of detection or identification. A detailed forensic investigation report must be submitted within 14 days.

SEED-CYBERSECURITY-section_20_security_o.raw_text: Banks must operate a 24x7 Security Operations Center (SOC) to monitor, detect, and respond to potential threats in real-time. The SOC must perform automated log analysis of all critical infrastructure, firewall logs, and active directory activities.

SEED-KYC_BASELINE_2024-paragraph_38_cust.section_title: Paragraph 38 - Customer Due Diligence for Individuals
